query: I need each regional sales manager to only see their own region's rows when they open our shared Sales.pbix. How do I set that up?
why: Classic per-user row restriction in a shared model = RLS, target skill.

query: set up dynamic rls using userprincipalname so reps only see their accounts based on a UserEmail column in DimSalesperson
why: Dynamic RLS with USERPRINCIPALNAME() is explicitly the target skill's core.

query: our HR model has a Salary column in DimEmployee that only the comp team should ever see, but everyone uses the same report. how do i hide just that column?
why: Hiding sensitive columns is object-level security (OLS), covered by this skill.

query: A manager needs to see sales for their whole team including everyone reporting under them down the org chart. How do I build that security in Power BI?
why: Hierarchical manager-sees-team security via bridge/security table is in-scope.

query: after i published the report one user says they see no data at all and another says they can see every region. roles are configured. whats wrong?
why: Diagnosing user sees all/no data is an explicit RLS troubleshooting trigger.

query: how do i apply microsoft purview sensitivity labels like Confidential to our finance semantic model?
why: Sensitivity labels / information protection are listed in scope.

query: should i build one model with RLS roles per country or just publish a separate pbix for each country team?
why: Static-vs-dynamic / RLS-vs-separate-models decision is the skill's first core decision.

query: my rls role works in desktop with View As but the totals look broken in the service because I have a bidirectional relationship between DimRegion and FactSales
why: RLS behaving unexpectedly with bidirectional relationships is an explicit trigger.

query: we want to pass a tenant id through CUSTOMDATA from our embedded app so each customer only sees their own rows
why: CUSTOMDATA()-based row filtering for multitenancy is an uncommon-but-valid RLS case.

query: how do I test that my Midwest role actually filters correctly before I publish? want to verify what a Midwest user would see
why: Testing roles with View As is explicitly part of this skill.

query: I'm rolling out our app to the sales workspace and need to assign Viewer vs Member roles and decide who gets the app vs direct workspace access
why: Workspace roles / app distribution is publishing-and-sharing-content, not data-row RLS.

query: set the cross-filter direction between DimDate and FactSales to single and fix the cardinality on my star schema relationships
why: Relationships/cardinality/cross-filter setup is modeling-semantic-data.

query: the tenant admin needs to lock down who can export data and turn off publish-to-web across the org, plus set up audit logging
why: Tenant settings, export controls and auditing are administering-and-governing.

query: write a DAX measure that calculates net margin % and only counts active customers using CALCULATE with a filter
why: Authoring a CALCULATE measure is authoring-dax-measures, not a security filter rule.

query: my big 4GB model is slow and refreshes time out, how do I reduce size and find the bottleneck with Performance Analyzer?
why: Model size/performance tuning is optimizing-model-performance.

query: I want to filter the report by region using a slicer so users can pick their own region from a dropdown on the dashboard
why: Interactive slicer-based filtering (user-driven, not security) is building-interactive-reports.

query: promote my report from the dev workspace to test then prod using deployment pipelines without breaking the data source bindings
why: Dev-to-prod promotion is managing-deployment-lifecycle.

query: how do I add a Key Influencers visual to understand what drives customer churn in my retention dashboard?
why: AI visual selection is ai-powered-analysis-selection.

query: connecting to our Azure SQL warehouse, should I use DirectQuery or Import for the FactSales table and how do I set up the gateway?
why: Connector / storage mode / gateway is connecting-data-sources.

query: I need a pixel-perfect printable invoice report grouped by customer that exports cleanly to PDF for each account manager
why: Pixel-perfect printable RDL output is building-paginated-reports (per-user delivery is not RLS).
